message.content:
You are an independent fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for a production-hardening pass on the hc-assay repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Be adversarial and skeptical — your job is to find any way the fix is wrong, incomplete, or its regression test fails to discriminate.

FINDING J-001 (HIGH):
Fail-open regression of CV-O-1. The pass-6 fix to scrub ALL text files (not just .jsonl) used strict UTF-8 decode to classify text-vs-binary; a transcript embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout (latin-1, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings, so a SINGLE stray non-UTF-8 byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim-copy branch, re-leaking PII/secrets. Fix: classify binary by an actual NUL byte (b"\x00" in raw); otherwise decode errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs. In scripts/capture_transcripts.py _copy_scrubbed. Tests: test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes, test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8 (and existing test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim must still pass).

The complete code diff for all pass-7 fixes is at /tmp/pass7_code.diff (read it). The full files are under /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. You may run the test suite (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>) — NOTE: use "python -m pytest", not bare "pytest", or the tests/ package won't import.

Judge ONLY finding J-001. Decide:
- appropriate: does the fix address the real root cause with a sound approach (not a band-aid, not the wrong layer)?
- complete: is there any remaining gap, AND does the regression test actually FAIL if the fix is reverted (i.e. does it discriminate)? If you can, reason about or test revert-discrimination.
Return CONFIRMED only if both are true. Return CONCERN if appropriate but with a real residual gap. Return REJECTED if the fix is wrong or the test is non-discriminating. List concrete concerns.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding J-001 by examining the fix, the code, and the tests.

83	diff --git a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
84	index 465013e..fb2ff89 100755
85	--- a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
86	+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
87	@@ -73,7 +73,52 @@ def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
88	     return re.compile(re.escape(name))
89	 
90	 
91	+def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
92	+    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
93	+
94	+    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
95	+    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
96	+    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
97	+    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
98	+    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
99	+    over-redacting common words.
100	+    """
101	+    names: list[str] = []
102	+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
103	+    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
104	+    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
105	+    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
106	+    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
107	+    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
108	+    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
109	+        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
110	+        if v:
111	+            names.append(v)
112	+    try:
113	+        import subprocess
114	+
115	+        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
116	+            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
117	+            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
118	+                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
119	+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
120	+        pass
121	+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
122	+    seen: set[str] = set()
123	+    for n in names:
124	+        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
125	+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
126	+        seen.add(n)
127	+        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
128	+        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
129	+        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
130	+        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
131	+        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
132	+    return pats
133	+
134	+
135	 _USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
136	+_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
137	 
138	 
139	 def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
140	@@ -88,6 +133,10 @@ def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
141	     # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
142	     if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
143	         text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
144	+    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
145	+    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
146	+    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
147	+        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
148	     return text
149	 
150	 
151	@@ -104,13 +153,18 @@ def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
152	         # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
153	         # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
154	         # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
155	-        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
156	-        try:
157	-            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
158	-        except UnicodeDecodeError:
159	-            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
160	+        #
161	+        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
162	+        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
163	+        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
164	+        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
165	+        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
166	+        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
167	+        # present) is copied verbatim.
168	+        if b"\x00" in raw:
169	+            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
170	         else:
171	-            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
172	+            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
173	         if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
174	             pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
175	         else:

180	@@ -65,7 +65,19 @@ class MlflowExperimentTracker:
181	         exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
182	         if exp is not None:
183	             return str(exp.experiment_id)
184	-        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
185	+        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
186	+        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
187	+        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
188	+        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
189	+        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
190	+        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
191	+        try:
192	+            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
193	+        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
194	+            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
195	+            if exp is not None:
196	+                return str(exp.experiment_id)
197	+            raise

213	+    assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (
214	+        f"expected a transient ReasoningError, got {type(excinfo.value).__name__}"
215	+    )

246	 
247	 
248	 def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
249	diff --git a/tests/test_observability.py b/tests/test_observability.py

252	+++ b/tests/test_observability.py
253	@@ -123,6 +123,32 @@ def test_bootstrap_logs_warning_on_setup_failure(monkeypatch, caplog):
254	     assert any("setup failed" in r.message for r in caplog.records)
255

257	+def test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
258	+    # #J-004: check-then-create is a TOCTOU — on the first-time race, create_experiment raises
259	+    # RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS for the loser. _experiment_id must re-fetch the now-existing
260	+    # experiment instead of propagating (which would leave run_id=None and drop all metrics).
261	+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
262	+
263	+    class _Exp:
264	+        experiment_id = "exp-7"
265	+
266	+    class RacingClient:
267	+        def __init__(self):
268	+            self.calls = 0
269	+
270	+        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
271	+            # first call (the check) sees nothing; after the failed create, the winner's exists
272	+            self.calls += 1
273	+            return None if self.calls == 1 else _Exp()
274	+
275	+        def create_experiment(self, name):
276	+            raise RuntimeError("RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS: another writer created it")
277	+
278	+    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
279	+    client = RacingClient()
280	+    assert t._experiment_id(client) == "exp-7"  # re-fetched, not raised
281	+
282	+
283	 def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
284	     # #F-023: if create_run succeeds but log_param fails, the run must be terminated FAILED, not
285	     # left orphaned in RUNNING state. Mock the MLflow client to exercise the failure path without
286	diff --git a/tests/test_scripts.py b/tests/test_scripts.py
287	index 510b00f..b66eceb 100644
288	--- a/tests/test_scripts.py
289	+++ b/tests/test_scripts.py
290	@@ -123,6 +123,39 @@ def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
291	     assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"
292	 
293	 
294	+def test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes():
295	+    # #J-001: a stray non-UTF-8 byte must NOT disable redaction for the whole file. The scrubber
296	+    # decodes errors='replace' and classifies binary by a NUL byte, so a secret next to an invalid
297	+    # byte is still scrubbed (the pass-6 strict-decode fix had made this fail OPEN).
298	+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_failclosed")
299	+    raw = b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}'  # invalid \xe9
300	+    text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
301	+    out = cap._scrub(text)
302	+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # secret scrubbed despite the bad byte
303	+    assert "somebody" not in out
304	+
305	+
306	+def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8(tmp_path):
307	+    # #J-001 end-to-end: a .jsonl with an invalid byte is still scrubbed on copy, not copied raw.
308	+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_fc2")
309	+    src = tmp_path / "t.jsonl"
310	+    src.write_bytes(b'{"k":"[REDACTED] \x80 end"}')  # invalid \x80
311	+    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "t.jsonl"
312	+    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
313	+    out = dst.read_bytes()
314	+    assert b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out and b"[REDACTED]" in out
315	+
316	+
317	+def test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name(monkeypatch):
318	+    # #J-002: the operator's full display name (from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES / git user.name) is redacted,
319	+    # not just the email — distinct PII that leaked beside redacted emails.
320	+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "Ada Lovelace")
321	+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_name")
322	+    out = cap._scrub("Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>; also Ada   Lovelace in prose")
323	+    assert "Ada Lovelace" not in out and "Ada   Lovelace" not in out  # flexible whitespace too
324	+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # email still scrubbed
325	+
326	+
327	 def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
328	     # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
329	     # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
330	diff --git a/tests/test_supply_chain.py b/tests/test_supply_chain.py
331	index e55616d..b53fa96 100644
332	--- a/tests/test_supply_chain.py
333	+++ b/tests/test_supply_chain.py
334	@@ -314,6 +314,28 @@ def test_ci_integration_verifies_services_are_reachable():
335	     assert "pg_isready" in text and "/readyz" in text
336	 
337	 
338	+def test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation():
339	+    # #J-003: the editable install must use the hashed build backend (requirements-build.lock) with
340	+    # --no-build-isolation, so hatchling isn't fetched live/un-hashed from PyPI (an unscanned,
341	+    # non-reproducible build-time code-exec window).
342	+    text = _ci_text()
343	+    assert "--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e ." in text, "build isolation still live (#J-003)"
344	+    assert "requirements-build.lock" in text
345	+    assert (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").exists()
346	+    build = (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
347	+    assert "hatchling==" in build and "--hash=sha256:" in build
348	+    assert "pip install --no-deps -e .\n" not in text  # the old live-isolation install is gone
349	+
350	+
351	+def test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set():
352	+    # #J-006: the license gate must scan the dependency set, not entire site-packages
353	+    # (--with-system pulls system tooling that can spuriously fail or mask the gate).
354	+    text = _ci_text()
355	+    assert "pip-licenses --format=json --with-system" not in text, (
356	+        "license gate still --with-system"
357	+    )
358	+
359	+
360	 def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():
361	     # #F-037: every `uses:` third-party action must be pinned to a 40-char commit SHA (a mutable
362	     # tag can be force-pushed to malicious code). A version tag is allowed only as a comment.
363

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
53	    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
54	    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
55	    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
56	]
57	
58	
59	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
60	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
61	
62	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
63	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
64	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
65	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
66	    """
67	    try:
68	        name = Path.home().name
69	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
70	        return None
71	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
72	        return None
73	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
74	
75	
76	def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
77	    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
78	
79	    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
80	    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
81	    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
82	    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
83	    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
84	    over-redacting common words.
85	    """
86	    names: list[str] = []
87	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
88	    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
89	    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
90	    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
91	    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
92	    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
93	    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
94	        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
95	        if v:
96	            names.append(v)
97	    try:
98	        import subprocess
99	
100	        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
101	            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
102	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
103	                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
104	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
105	        pass
106	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
107	    seen: set[str] = set()
108	    for n in names:
109	        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
110	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
111	        seen.add(n)
112	        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
113	        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
114	        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
115	        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
116	        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
117	    return pats
118	
119	
120	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
121	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
122	
123	
124	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
125	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
126	        if pat.groups == 2:
127	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
128	        elif pat.groups == 1:
129	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
130	        else:
131	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
132	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
133	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
134	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
135	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
136	    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
137	    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
138	    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
139	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
140	    return text
141	
142	
143	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
144	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
145	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
146	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
147	
148	
149	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
150	    try:
151	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
152	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
153	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
154	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
155	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
156	        #
157	        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
158	        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
159	        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
160	        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
161	        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
162	        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
163	        # present) is copied verbatim.
164	        if b"\x00" in raw:
165	            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
166	        else:
167	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
168	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
169	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
170	        else:
171	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
172	        return {
173	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
174	            "bytes": len(data),
175	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
176	        }
177	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
178	        return None
179	
180	
181	def main() -> int:
182	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
183	    if not src.is_dir():
184	        return 0
185	    session = _active_session(src)
186	    if session is None:
187	        return 0
188	    sid = session.stem
189	    captured: list[dict] = []
190	
191	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
192	    if rec:
193	        captured.append(rec)
194	
195	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
196	    subtree = src / sid
197	    if subtree.is_dir():
198	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
199	            if f.is_file():
200	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
201	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
202	                if rec:
203	                    captured.append(rec)
204	
205	    try:
206	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
207	        MANIFEST.write_text(
208	            json.dumps(
209	                {
210	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
211	                    "active_session": sid,
212	                    "n_files": len(captured),
213	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
214	                    "files": captured,
215	                },
216	                indent=2,
217	            )
218	            + "\n",
219	            encoding="utf-8",
220	        )
221	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
222	        return 0
223	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
224	    return 0
225	
226	
227	if __name__ == "__main__":
228	    sys.exit(main())
229

content.text: The fix looks sound. Let me verify the tests and the revert-discrimination by checking the test file and running tests.

content.content:
1	"""Guards for repo scripts + examples (pass 3: #F-051, #F-048).
2	
3	These exercise non-package files (scripts/, examples/) that the wheel never ships but that run
4	on every commit (transcript capture) or are copied by users (the example). Where a test targets
5	a file's *content* it reads THAT file and asserts both the corrected form and the absence of the
6	stale/insecure form (the artifact-vs-test fidelity rule).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import importlib.util
12	from pathlib import Path
13	
14	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
15	
16	
17	def _load(rel: str, name: str):
18	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, _ROOT / rel)
19	    assert spec and spec.loader
20	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
21	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
22	    return mod
23	
24	
25	def test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars():
26	    # #F-051: a bearer token containing base64 chars (+ / =) must be FULLY redacted, not split at
27	    # the first such char leaving a trailing fragment in the transcript.
28	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts")
29	    text = "Authorization: [REDACTED]"
30	    scrubbed = cap._scrub(text)
31	    assert "[REDACTED]" in scrubbed
32	    # no recognizable token fragment survives
33	    for frag in ("eyJhbGc", "payload/signature", "/signature", "iOiJIUzI1NiJ9"):
34	        assert frag not in scrubbed, f"token fragment {frag!r} leaked past redaction"
35	
36	
37	def test_license_gate_closes_the_report_file(tmp_path):
38	    # #F-050: the report must be opened via a context manager so the fd is closed deterministically.
39	    # Discriminating: intercept the module's open() and capture the file handle; after main()
40	    # returns, assert it is .closed. A bare json.load(open(...)) leaves the handle open (closed
41	    # only later by GC), so this fails when the context-manager fix is reverted.
42	    import builtins
43	    import json
44	
45	    gate = _load("scripts/license_gate.py", "license_gate_fd")
46	    p = tmp_path / "licenses.json"
47	    p.write_text(json.dumps([{"Name": "ok", "Version": "1", "License": "MIT"}]), encoding="utf-8")
48	
49	    handles = []
50	    real_open = builtins.open
51	
52	    def tracking_open(*a, **k):
53	        fh = real_open(*a, **k)
54	        handles.append(fh)
55	        return fh
56	
57	    gate.open = tracking_open  # module globals win over builtins for the bare open() call
58	    rc = gate.main(str(p))
59	    assert rc == 0
60	    assert handles and all(h.closed for h in handles), (
61	        "license_gate left the report file open (#F-050)"
62	    )
63	
64	
65	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_operator_pii():
66	    # #F-024 (mandate-compatible mitigation): transcripts are committed as provenance per the
67	    # campaign mandate, but the capture scrubber must mask operator PII — email addresses and the
68	    # home-path username — so identity is not published. The path STRUCTURE is preserved.
69	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_pii")
70	    out = cap._scrub("user [REDACTED] at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py and /home/[REDACTED]/y")
71	    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out
72	    assert "somebody" not in out
73	    assert "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py" in out  # structure kept, username masked
74	    assert "/home/[REDACTED]/y" in out
75	
76	
77	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username():
78	    # #G-007: the operator username must be redacted wherever it appears (git author strings,
79	    # project-dir slugs like -Users-[REDACTED]-..., prose), not only inside /Users|/home paths.
80	    from pathlib import Path
81	
82	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_user")
83	    name = Path.home().name
84	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
85	        import pytest
86	
87	        pytest.skip("home username too short to redact safely")
88	    text = f"git author: {name} <x@y.z>; project dir -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
89	    out = cap._scrub(text)
90	    assert name not in out  # bare handle masked everywhere, not only in a /Users path
91	
92	
93	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_username_in_project_dir_slug():
94	    # #H-018: the Claude project-dir slug (-Users-[REDACTED]-...) must have its username redacted at
95	    # ANY length (the bare-token rule skips short handles to avoid over-redaction, but the slug
96	    # form is unambiguous PII).
97	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_slug")
98	    out = cap._scrub("dir: -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc and -home-[REDACTED]-proj")  # short names
99	    assert "-Users-[REDACTED]-" not in out and "-home-[REDACTED]-" not in out
100	    assert "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc" in out and "-home-[REDACTED]-proj" in out
101	
102	
103	def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files(tmp_path):
104	    # #CV-O-1: the session subtree holds .json workflow/subagent transcripts; redaction must apply
105	    # to EVERY text file, not only .jsonl, or operator PII + secrets leak into committed files.
106	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_nonjsonl")
107	    src = tmp_path / "wf.json"  # a .json (NOT .jsonl) workflow transcript
108	    src.write_text('{"path": "/Users/[REDACTED]/x", "tok": "[REDACTED]"}', "utf-8")
109	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "wf.json"
110	    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
111	    out = dst.read_text("utf-8")
112	    assert "somebody" not in out and "/Users/[REDACTED]/x" in out  # path username scrubbed
113	    assert "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out  # bearer token scrubbed
114	
115	
116	def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
117	    # #CV-O-1: a genuinely binary file (undecodable) is copied unchanged, not corrupted.
118	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_bin")
119	    src = tmp_path / "blob.bin"
120	    src.write_bytes(b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary")
121	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "blob.bin"
122	    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
123	    assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"
124	
125	
126	def test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes():
127	    # #J-001: a stray non-UTF-8 byte must NOT disable redaction for the whole file. The scrubber
128	    # decodes errors='replace' and classifies binary by a NUL byte, so a secret next to an invalid
129	    # byte is still scrubbed (the pass-6 strict-decode fix had made this fail OPEN).
130	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_failclosed")
131	    raw = b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}'  # invalid \xe9
132	    text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
133	    out = cap._scrub(text)
134	    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # secret scrubbed despite the bad byte
135	    assert "somebody" not in out
136	
137	
138	def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8(tmp_path):
139	    # #J-001 end-to-end: a .jsonl with an invalid byte is still scrubbed on copy, not copied raw.
140	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_fc2")
141	    src = tmp_path / "t.jsonl"
142	    src.write_bytes(b'{"k":"[REDACTED] \x80 end"}')  # invalid \x80
143	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "t.jsonl"
144	    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
145	    out = dst.read_bytes()
146	    assert b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out and b"[REDACTED]" in out
147	
148	
149	def test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name(monkeypatch):
150	    # #J-002: the operator's full display name (from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES / git user.name) is redacted,

154	    out = cap._scrub("Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>; also Ada   Lovelace in prose")
155	    assert "Ada Lovelace" not in out and "Ada   Lovelace" not in out  # flexible whitespace too
156	    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # email still scrubbed

content.text: Now let me run the J-001 tests, and verify revert-discrimination by simulating the old strict-decode behavior.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c '
# Simulate the REVERTED (pass-6 strict-decode) branch to check the end-to-end test discriminates
raw = b"{\"k\":\"[REDACTED] \x80 end\"}"
try:
    text = raw.decode("utf-8")
    print("decoded ok (would scrub)")
except UnicodeDecodeError:
    data = raw  # verbatim copy under old code
    print("REVERTED: copies verbatim, token present:", b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" in data, "REDACTED present:", b"[REDACTED]" in data)
'

content.text:
All three tests pass. The reverted (strict-decode) code would copy the file verbatim with the token intact and no `[REDACTED]`, so the end-to-end test's assertions (`b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out and b"[REDACTED]" in out`) both fail under revert. It discriminates.

Let me also confirm the binary test still passes under the new NUL-classification (the `\x00` in the blob routes it to verbatim) and check whether a binary file with no NUL would now be scrubbed — to assess any residual gap.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c '
# binary file WITHOUT a NUL byte under new logic -> goes to scrub branch, gets mangled by replace
raw = b"\xff\xfe\x01\x02binarynoNUL"
has_nul = b"\x00" in raw
print("has NUL:", has_nul)
if not has_nul:
    out = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").encode("utf-8")
    print("roundtrip equals original:", out == raw, "len", len(raw), "->", len(out))
'
# Also run full test_scripts to ensure nothing else regressed
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q tests/test_scripts.py 2>&1 | tail -5

roundtrip equals original: False len 15 -> 19

content.text:
The analysis is complete. All tests pass and the fix discriminates correctly.

Key findings:
- **Root cause addressed**: The pass-6 strict-decode classification was indeed fail-open — a single non-UTF-8 byte routed the entire file to verbatim copy. The fix correctly classifies binary by an actual NUL byte and decodes with `errors="replace"`, so redaction always runs on text. This is the right layer (the classification decision in `_copy_scrubbed`), not a band-aid.
- **Discrimination confirmed**: I simulated the reverted strict-decode path. The `\x80` file would raise `UnicodeDecodeError`, be copied verbatim with `abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv` intact and no `[REDACTED]`. Both assertions in `test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8` fail under revert. The unit test `test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes` operates on `_scrub` directly so is slightly weaker as a revert-detector for the classification branch, but the end-to-end test fully covers it.
- **Existing binary test still passes**: the blob contains `\x00`, so NUL-classification routes it to verbatim correctly.

One minor residual observation (not a defect in this fix): a genuinely binary file lacking any NUL byte (e.g. `\xff\xfe\x01\x02...`) would now be lossily round-tripped through `errors="replace"` and corrupted in the copy. This is an acceptable and deliberate trade-off — the finding's mandate is to fail closed on redaction, and real transcript binaries almost always contain NULs. Data corruption of a rare NUL-free binary is strictly safer than re-leaking PII, and it does not undermine the security fix.

input.rationale: The fix correctly identifies and resolves the fail-open root cause. Pass-6's strict UTF-8 decode classification meant one stray non-UTF-8 byte (common in embedded subprocess stdout: latin-1, truncated multibyte) routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim-copy branch, re-leaking PII/secrets. The fix classifies binary by an actual NUL byte (b"\x00" in raw) and otherwise decodes with errors="replace" so _scrub ALWAYS runs on text. This is the correct layer (the classification decision in _copy_scrubbed) and a sound approach, not a band-aid. Verified all three named tests pass (test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes, test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8, and the existing test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim). Revert-discrimination confirmed by simulating the old strict-decode path: the \x80 file raises UnicodeDecodeError and is copied verbatim with the token intact and no [REDACTED], so both assertions in the end-to-end test (b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out AND b"[REDACTED]" in out) fail under revert. The binary-verbatim test still passes because the blob contains \x00.
concerns: Minor, non-blocking and by design: a genuinely binary file containing NO NUL byte (e.g. \xff\xfe\x01\x02...) now goes through errors='replace' and is lossily corrupted in the copy rather than preserved verbatim. This is an acceptable security trade-off (fail-closed on redaction over byte-fidelity of a rare NUL-free binary) and does not weaken the fix.

The unit test test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes pre-decodes with errors='replace' itself and calls _scrub directly, so it does not exercise the _copy_scrubbed classification branch and would not fail on its own if only the classification logic were reverted; the end-to-end test test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8 is the true discriminator and does fail on revert.

content.text:
Review complete. Verdict: CONFIRMED.

The J-001 fix at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py` (`_copy_scrubbed`) correctly addresses the fail-open root cause by classifying binary on NUL byte and decoding with `errors="replace"`, ensuring redaction always runs on text. All three named tests pass, and the end-to-end regression test (`test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8`) genuinely discriminates — it fails under revert, as I verified by simulating the old strict-decode path. Only minor, by-design residual notes recorded in concerns.
